item.title: Saturday MEME Phone Clearing Dump (#420)
item.description:
1. Wow so realistic
2. I'm in this image and I don't like it
3. Stellar Blade just added a new update, can you guess what they fixed?
4
5. TELL ME
6. How did you mean it?
7. The mask stays on
8. Meanwhile in cultured nations
9. How many of you will watch Galadriel Twitch streams?
10
11
12
13. Ballsketbat
14. Stop scrolling and read this
https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: Maggie Smith – a life in pictures
item.description:
https://www.theguardian.com/film/gallery/2024/sep/27/maggie-smith-a-life-in-pictures
Smith with George Nader in the Ealing Studios film Nowhere to Go, 1958
With Peter Ustinov in Hot Millions, 1968
A still from The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie, 1969
Oh! What a Lovely War, 1969
Travels With My Aunt, 1972
Maggie Smith with her husband, actor Robert Stephens, and children Christopher and Toby in 1972. Smith and Stephens divorced in 1975
With Michael Caine in California Suite, 1978
Smith, second from left, in the Merchant Ivory film A Room With a View, 1986
With Michael Palin in A Private Function, 1984
In a scene from The Secret Garden, 1993
Tea with Mussolini, 1999
With Kelly MacDonald in Gosford Park, 2001
As Professor Minerva McGonagall in Harry Potter and the Philosopher’s Stone, 2001
With Judi Dench in The Breath of Life, at Theatre Royal Haymarket, 2002
The Best Exotic Marigold Hotel, 2011
Smith in the final series of Downton Abbey, 2015
Alex Jennings and Smith as Alan Bennett and Miss Shepherd in The Lady in the Van, 2015
Winning the best actress award for The Lady in the Van at the London Evening Standard film awards in 2016
The Miracle Club, 2023
Photograph: Jane Bown/The Observer
item.dc:creator: GlobalImageServiceCat

item.title: It's been long enough, time to cut the shit.
item.description: I've been working on myself for a long time. I quit drinking 5 years ago but weed (edibles) has always been a crutch for me. The problem is I'm a "0/100" kinda guy: I don't know how to do ANYTHING in moderation. So for a long time I've been eating my edibles on a daily basis, literally planning my days and weekends around it. And the worst part? I've been doing it behind my wife and kids backs: they had no idea I was completely stoned all these years (I finally shared my need to compartmentalize due to a rough childhood with my therapist and this is part of it). Well, I'm almost 45 and, well, it's been taking a toll on my professional productivity and if there's one thing I won't stand for it's my career taking a hit (get it: "taking a hit"?!!!....GET IT?!!!). So I finished my edibles last week and I refuse to go buy more, and folks I think this is it. The future is a bit stressful for me. A fair percentage of my cannabis use was to numb myself from a few things I didn't want to address. One example is my wife and kids (sounds bad, but hear me out): my wife - whom I love very much - is an extreme introvert and homebody, and well, I'm a social butterfly. Being stoned all the time has allowed me to avoid being angry at a low activity and barely social lifestyle. So I am worried that there will be some conflict in the future but right now I need to keep focused on myself. Anyway, I've been feeling great and am hoping I can finally shed this dependency in conjunction with ending the lying to my family. I think I can.
item.dc:creator: DamnedIfYouDo

item.title: Images of Note: Please let me go back to hell
item.description:
I remember when Stallman was considered a loveable eccentric, now...yeesh.
sus
they share a meal
Imagine being too racist for Rhodesia. Whenever some right winger gets kicked out of a notoriously racist group for being too racist I'm like "fuck me there's limits for these fuckers?"
JK Rowling would be like the Dursleys if she had a magical orphan nephew.
owo
:3
Oh no
